name: Maple Chicken Breakfast Sausage
description: A slightly sweet and savory homemade sausage made with ground chicken and warm spices.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, add ground chicken, maple syrup, salt, sage, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, thyme, red pepper flakes, nutmeg, and cloves.
Mix thoroughly using your hands or a spoon for about 1–2 minutes until all ingredients are evenly combined.
Form the mixture into small patties (about 2–3 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick) so they cook evenly.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with oil.
Place the patties in the skillet in a single layer without overcrowding.
Cook for about 4–5 minutes on the first side until browned, then flip and cook another 4–5 minutes until fully cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Remove from the skillet and let rest for 2–3 minutes before serving.
Serve warm.

name: Beef Tips and Gravy
description: Tender beef tips simmered in a rich, flavorful brown gravy.
instructions: Cut the beef into 1-inch cubes and remove any large pieces of fat. Pat dry with paper towels and season lightly with salt and black pepper.
Sprinkle flour over the beef and toss until all pieces are evenly coated. This helps create a thicker gravy later.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat and add olive oil. Once hot, add the beef in batches in a single layer.
Sear each side for about 30–45 seconds until browned but not fully cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add red wine to the pan. Use a spatula to scrape up browned bits from the bottom (this adds flavor). Let it simmer for 2–3 minutes until reduced by half.
Add butter and diced onion. Cook for about 5 minutes until softened and slightly golden.
Add garlic and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, onion powder, and garlic powder. Stir well and bring to a boil.
Return the beef (and any juices) to the pan. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 1 hour until the beef is tender and the gravy has thickened.
Stir occasionally during cooking to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.
Remove from heat and let sit for 5 minutes before serving so the gravy thickens slightly more.
Serve over mashed potatoes, rice, or noodles.

name: Breaded Steak (make with Amogio Sauce)
description: A crispy, pan-fried steak coated in seasoned breadcrumbs for a flavorful, golden crust.
instructions: Place the steak between plastic wrap or parchment paper and pound it with a meat mallet or heavy pan until it is about 3/4 to 1 inch thick so it cooks evenly.
Season both sides of the steak with salt and black pepper.
Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one with breadcrumbs.
Dredge each steak in the flour, making sure it is fully coated, then shake off excess.
Dip the steak into the eggs, coating both sides completely.
Press the steak into the breadcrumbs, ensuring an even coating across the entire surface.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add oil until it reaches about 350°F.
Place the breaded steak into the pan and cook for about 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the pan and place on a wire rack or paper towels to drain excess oil.
Let rest for 2–3 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Tenderloin
description: A simple, elegant roast with garlic herb butter and a quick sear-and-roast method for perfect tenderness.
instructions: Remove the beef tenderloin from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 1–2 hours before cooking so it cooks evenly. Pat it dry with paper towels and season all sides generously with salt and black pepper.
Preheat oven to 425°F and place a rack in the center. Use a large oven-safe skillet (10–12 inch cast iron works best) or roasting pan.
In a small bowl, mix butter, garlic, rosemary, and thyme until fully combined to create a herb butter. Set aside.

Heat the skillet over medium-high heat and add a small amount of oil. Once hot, place the tenderloin in the pan and sear for about 8–10 minutes total, turning every 2–3 minutes until all sides are browned and a crust forms.
Remove the pan briefly from heat and spread the herb butter evenly over the entire surface of the meat.
Transfer the skillet directly into the oven and roast uncovered for 10–20 minutes, depending on thickness and desired doneness. Use a thermometer for accuracy:
120–125°F for medium-rare
130–135°F for medium
Remove from the oven when the internal temperature is about 5°F below your target (it will continue cooking while resting).
Transfer the meat to a cutting board, loosely cover with foil, and let rest for 15–20 minutes to retain juices.
Slice into 1-inch thick pieces and serve.

name: Beef Burgundy Over Noodles
description: A rich, slow-simmered beef dish with mushrooms and wine, served over tender noodles.
instructions: Heat a large pot or Dutch oven (4–6 quarts) over medium-high heat. Add the beef and cook for about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides.
Add the diced onion and cook for another 3–4 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Stir in the mushrooms, butter, condensed soup, and Burgundy wine. Mix well until everything is evenly combined.
Season with salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover with a lid, and cook for about 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
While the beef finishes cooking, prepare egg noodles according to package directions in a 3–4 quart pot. Drain well.
Serve the beef mixture over the cooked egg noodles.
Let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving to allow the sauce to settle slightly.

name: Poor Man’s Prime Rib
description: A budget-friendly roast that mimics prime rib using a simple high-heat method for a tender, juicy result.
instructions: Preheat oven to 500°F and place a rack in the center. Use a roasting pan or baking dish large enough to hold the roast without crowding.
In a small bowl, combine salt, black pepper, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika. Mix thoroughly.
Pat the roast dry with paper towels, then rub the seasoning mixture evenly over all sides, pressing it in so it sticks well.
Place the roast in the roasting pan with no lid and no added liquid.
Put the pan into the oven and immediately reduce the temperature to 475°F.
Cook for 7 minutes per pound (about 21 minutes for a 3 lb roast). Do not open the oven door during this time.

After the cooking time is complete, turn off the oven completely and leave the roast inside for 2 to 2 1/2 hours. Do NOT open the oven door at all during this time—this step is critical for proper cooking.
Remove the roast from the oven. The internal temperature should be about 145°F for medium.
Transfer to a cutting board and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing thinly against the grain.

name: Asian Green Bean Turkey Rice Bowl
description: A quick and flavorful stir-fry with ground turkey, green beans, and a savory Asian-style sauce.
instructions: Add rice to a medium pot (2–3 quarts) with water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low, cover, and simmer for about 15–18 minutes until tender.
While the rice cooks, heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over high heat and add sesame oil. Once hot, add green onions and garlic and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring frequently until fragrant.
Add the ground turkey to the skillet. Cook for 3–5 minutes, breaking it apart with a spatula, until mostly cooked through and no longer pink.
Stir in chili garlic sauce, hoisin sauce, and ginger. Mix well so the meat is evenly coated.
Add the green beans and soy sauce. Stir everything together and continue cooking over high heat for 7–9 minutes, stirring often, until the green beans are slightly tender but still crisp.
Add rice vinegar and cook for 1 more minute, stirring to combine all flavors.
Serve the turkey and green bean mixture over the cooked rice.

name: Artichoke Chicken
description: A one-pan baked chicken dish with artichokes, lemon, garlic, and a light white wine sauce.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F and use a large oven-safe skillet or baking dish (10–12 inch) so everything cooks evenly.
Slice each chicken breast in half lengthwise to make thinner cutlets, then pound them to about 1/4 inch thick using a meat mallet or heavy pan. This helps them cook evenly and stay juicy.
In a small bowl, mix oregano, thyme, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Pat the chicken dry and season both sides evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat the skillet over medium-high heat. Add butter and olive oil. Once melted and hot, add garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the white wine and let it simmer for 1–2 minutes to reduce slightly. Add lemon juice and capers, then turn off the heat.
Place the chicken cutlets into the skillet and nestle the artichoke hearts around them. Spoon some sauce over the top.
Cover and transfer to the oven. Bake for 10 minutes, then uncover and bake another 5–8 minutes until the chicken reaches 165°F internally.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 3–5 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve with the sauce spooned over the top.

name: Amogio Sauce
description: A fresh, garlicky tomato sauce with lemon and herbs, perfect for steak, chicken, or pasta.
instructions: Add the whole peeled tomatoes to a large mixing bowl. Use your hands to gently crush them into smaller chunks, leaving some texture instead of making it completely smooth.
Add the minced garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to the bowl.
Stir everything together thoroughly for about 1–2 minutes until all ingredients are evenly combined and coated.
Taste the sauce and adjust salt, pepper, or lemon juice if needed.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to blend together.
Remove from the refrigerator about 10–15 minutes before serving so it can come closer to room temperature (this improves flavor).
Serve over grilled meats, pasta, or bread.

name: Fusilli with Sausage, Artichokes, and Sun-Dried Tomatoes
description: A rich Italian pasta dish with sausage, artichokes, and a light wine-based sauce.
instructions: Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp of the reserved oil from the sun-dried tomatoes. Once hot, add the sausage and cook for about 8 minutes, breaking it into small pieces until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet over medium heat, add the artichoke hearts and garlic. Cook for about 2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic is fragrant and softened.
Pour in the chicken broth and white wine, then add the sun-dried tomatoes. Bring to a boil and cook for about 8 minutes until the liquid reduces slightly and forms a light sauce.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot (5–6 quarts) of salted water to a boil. Add the fusilli and cook for about 8 minutes until al dente (tender but still slightly firm). Drain without rinsing.
Add the cooked pasta and sausage back into the skillet with the sauce. Stir in parmesan cheese, basil, and parsley. Toss everything together for 1–2 minutes until the sauce coats the pasta.
Stir in the mozzarella (if using) and let it melt slightly from the heat.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving.
Serve warm with extra parmesan on top if desired.

name: Warm Couscous Salad with Apricot Vinaigrette
description: A light, sweet-and-savory couscous salad with apricot vinaigrette, herbs, and almonds.
instructions: Add chicken broth, butter, and 1/4 tsp salt to a medium saucepan (2–3 quarts) over high heat. Bring to a boil.
Once boiling, remove the pot from heat and immediately stir in the couscous. Cover with a tight-fitting lid and let sit for 5 minutes so the couscous absorbs the liquid.
After 5 minutes, uncover and fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains and prevent clumping.
In a small bowl, whisk together apricot preserves, olive oil, white wine vinegar, 1/4 tsp salt, and black pepper until smooth.
Pour about 3/4 of the vinaigrette over the warm couscous and stir until evenly coated. Taste and add more dressing if needed.
Add scallions, parsley (if using), and sliced almonds. Stir gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Let sit for a few minutes before serving. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: The Best Swedish Meatballs
description: Classic homemade Swedish meatballs in a rich, creamy gravy.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground beef, panko bread crumbs, parsley, allspice, nutmeg, onion, garlic powder, black pepper, salt, and egg. Mix gently until just combined so the meatballs stay tender.
Form the mixture into about 12 large meatballs or 18–20 smaller ones, rolling them evenly so they cook at the same rate.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil and 1 tbsp butter. Once hot, add the meatballs in a single layer.
Cook for about 7–8 minutes, turning frequently so all sides brown evenly. The internal temperature should reach 160°F. Remove and set aside on a plate.
In the same skillet over medium heat, add 4 tbsp butter. Once melted, whisk in the flour and cook for 2–3 minutes until it turns a light brown color (this removes the raw flour taste).
Slowly pour in the beef broth while whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Then add heavy cream, Worcestershire sauce, and Dijon mustard.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and cook for 3–5 minutes until it thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Return the meatballs to the skillet and spoon the sauce over them. Let them simmer for 2–3 minutes to soak up the flavor and heat through.
Remove from heat and let rest for a few minutes before serving. Serve over mashed potatoes or egg noodles.

name: Street Tacos
description: Simple and flavorful steak tacos with a citrus marinade and classic toppings.
instructions: Cut the steak into small bite-sized pieces (about 1-inch chunks) so it cooks quickly and evenly.
In a medium bowl, mix soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, lime juice, garlic, cilantro, chili powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper until fully combined.
Add the steak to the marinade and mix until all pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate for 1–2 hours to let the flavors soak in.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the steak along with the marinade.
Cook for about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the meat is browned and no longer pink (internal temp ~145°F for medium).
While the meat cooks, warm tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 20–30 seconds per side or until soft and slightly toasted.
Place the cooked steak in the center of each tortilla.
Serve immediately with additional toppings like diced onion, cilantro, or avocado if desired

name: Beef Fajitas
description: A classic grilled steak fajita recipe with a bold marinade and sautéed peppers and onions.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, tequila, smashed garlic, cilantro, 1 tbsp oil, Worcestershire sauce, oregano, red pepper flakes, cumin, and coriander until fully combined.
Place the steak in a large resealable bag or container and pour the marinade over it. Seal and refrigerate for at least 12 hours (up to 24 hours), turning occasionally so it marinates evenly.
Remove the steak from the marinade and pat it dry with paper towels. Season both sides with salt and black pepper.
Preheat a grill to high heat (or use a grill pan). Also preheat oven to 325°F to warm tortillas.
Wrap tortillas in foil and place in the oven for about 15 minutes until warm and soft.
Grill the steak for about 3–4 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp ~130–135°F). Adjust time based on desired doneness.
Remove the steak and let it rest on a cutting board for 10 minutes so the juices redistribute.

Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add the remaining 1 tbsp oil. Add the sliced peppers and onion. Cook for 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and slightly caramelized.
Add minced garlic, remaining salt, and pepper. Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain into strips.
Serve the steak and vegetables in the warm tortillas. Add lime juice or toppings if desired.

name: Strawberry Cheesecake Swirl
description: A creamy cheesecake with a graham cracker crust and sweet strawberry swirl throughout.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F and lightly grease a 9-inch springform pan. Wrap the outside of the pan with foil to prevent leaks if using a water bath.
In a bowl, mix graham cracker crumbs and melted butter until evenly coated. Press firmly into the bottom of the pan using the bottom of a cup. Set aside.

In a blender or food processor, blend strawberries until smooth. Pour into a small saucepan, add cornstarch and sugar, and cook over medium heat for about 5–7 minutes, stirring constantly, until thickened. Let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth using a hand mixer. Add sweetened condensed milk and mix until fully combined and creamy.
Add eggs and lemon juice, mixing on low speed just until combined. Do not overmix to avoid cracks in the cheesecake.
Pour half of the cheesecake filling over the crust. Drop spoonfuls of the strawberry sauce over the filling.
Add the remaining cheesecake filling, then add more spoonfuls of strawberry sauce on top. Use a knife or toothpick to gently swirl the sauce into the batter.
Place the pan on the center rack and bake for about 55–65 minutes, until the edges are set and the center slightly jiggles.
Turn off the oven, crack the door open, and let the cheesecake cool inside for 1 hour to prevent cracking.
Remove and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight before slicing.
Run a knife around the edge before removing the springform pan. Slice and serve chilled.

name: Sourdough Chicken Pot Pie
description: A creamy, hearty chicken pot pie topped with flaky sourdough biscuits.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, add flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, and baking soda. Stir until evenly combined.
Add the cold butter (cut into small pieces or grated). Use a fork or your hands to mix until the texture becomes crumbly, like coarse sand.
Add sourdough starter and milk. Stir just until combined. The dough will be slightly lumpy—do not overmix. Place the dough in the refrigerator while preparing the filling.

Heat a large skillet (10–12 inch or cast iron) over medium-low heat. Add butter, garlic, onion, potatoes, and carrots. Cook for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables begin to soften.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well. Cook for 2–3 minutes so the flour absorbs and removes any raw taste.
Pour in chicken broth and add parsley, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder. Stir, cover, and cook for about 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in peas, cooked chicken, and heavy cream. Mix until fully combined, then transfer the filling to a greased 9x13 inch baking dish.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Remove biscuit dough from the fridge and roll it out on a lightly floured surface to about 1/2 inch thick. Cut into biscuit shapes (about 10–12 pieces).
Place the biscuits evenly on top of the filling without overlapping too much.
Bake on the center rack for 10–15 minutes until the biscuits are golden brown and the filling is bubbling around the edges.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5–10 minutes before serving.

name: Creamy Garlic Shrimp
description: A quick and creamy shrimp dish with garlic, coconut milk, and bright lemon flavor.
instructions: Pat the shrimp dry with paper towels, then season lightly with salt and black pepper so they cook evenly and develop a good sear.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp ghee. Once hot and slightly shimmering, place the shrimp in a single layer.
Cook the shrimp for about 2 minutes per side until pink and opaque, then remove from the pan and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the remaining 1 tbsp ghee to the same skillet. Add garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly until fragrant but not browned.
Pour in the coconut milk, then add salt, black pepper, and nutritional yeast. Stir well and let the sauce simmer for about 3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Turn off the heat and return the shrimp to the skillet. Add lemon juice and gently toss to coat the shrimp in the sauce.
Sprinkle parsley and red pepper flakes on top and let sit for 1–2 minutes before serving.
Serve warm over rice, pasta, or vegetables.

name: Beef and Broccoli Freezer Meal
description: A simple freezer-friendly beef and broccoli recipe with a savory sauce and quick stovetop cooking.
instructions: Slice the steak into thin strips (about 1/4 inch thick) using a sharp knife. Cutting against the grain will make the meat more tender.
Place the sliced steak in a bowl and add cornstarch. Toss until all pieces are evenly coated. This helps create a light crust when cooking.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add sesame oil. Swirl the pan so the oil evenly coats the bottom.
Add the steak in a single layer. Season with salt and black pepper. Cook for 2–3 minutes without moving it so it browns, then flip and cook another 2–3 minutes until fully cooked.
Pour in the coconut aminos and stir continuously for about 1–2 minutes until the sauce coats the beef and slightly thickens.
Add the broccoli and a small splash of water (about 2–3 tbsp). Cover the skillet with a lid and cook for 3–4 minutes until the broccoli is bright green and slightly tender but still crisp.
Remove the lid and stir everything together. Cook for another 1–2 minutes to evaporate excess moisture and combine flavors.
Remove from heat and sprinkle sesame seeds over the top before serving.

name: Savory Mashed Sweet Potatoes
description: A flavorful twist on mashed sweet potatoes with herbs, garlic, and a creamy, savory finish.
instructions: Place the cubed sweet potatoes into a large pot (5–6 quarts) and add enough water to cover them by about 1 inch. Add 1 tsp salt.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce to medium and simmer uncovered for 9–12 minutes until the potatoes are fork-tender (a fork slides in easily).
Drain the potatoes thoroughly and return them to the pot. Mash using a potato masher until smooth or your desired texture.
Add most of the green onion and parsley (reserve a small amount for garnish), along with the garlic. Stir to combine.
Add sour cream, butter, and the remaining 1 tsp salt. Stir until the butter is fully melted and everything is creamy and evenly mixed.
Season with black pepper and taste. Add more salt if needed.
Transfer to a serving bowl. Swirl a small amount of extra sour cream on top if desired, then sprinkle with the reserved herbs and drizzle lightly with olive oil.
Serve warm.

name: Easy Pumpkin Pie
description: A simple, foolproof pumpkin pie made with just a few ingredients and one bowl.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F and place a rack in the center. Set your 9-inch pie dish on a baking sheet to make it easier to transfer in and out of the oven.
In a large mixing bowl, add pumpkin puree, sweetened condensed milk, eggs, and pumpkin pie spice. Whisk for about 1–2 minutes until the mixture is completely smooth and evenly combined.
Pour the filling into the unbaked pie crust, smoothing the top with a spatula so it bakes evenly.
Place the pie on the center rack and bake at 425°F for 15 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 350°F without removing the pie. Continue baking for 35–40 minutes, or until the center is set but still slightly jiggly.
To check doneness, insert a knife near the center—it should come out mostly clean.
Remove the pie from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for at least 2 hours so the filling fully sets.
For best texture, refrigerate for 1–2 hours before slicing. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

name: Pork Schnitzel
description: A classic crispy breaded pork cutlet, pan-fried until golden brown and served with lemon.
instructions: Place each pork chop between two sheets of plastic wrap and pound it with a meat mallet or heavy pan until it is about 1/4 inch thick. This helps it cook quickly and evenly.
Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour mixed with salt and pepper, one with eggs, cream, nutmeg, and lemon zest whisked together, and one with breadcrumbs mixed with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Dredge each piece of pork in the flour, making sure it is fully coated, then shake off any excess.
Dip the pork into the egg mixture, coating both sides completely.
Press the pork into the breadcrumbs, making sure the coating sticks evenly across the surface. Place breaded pieces on a plate or tray.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat and add oil and butter. Bring the temperature to about 350°F before cooking.
Add 1–2 schnitzels to the pan at a time, making sure they are not overlapping. Cook for 2–3 minutes on the first side until golden brown.
Flip and cook for another 2–3 minutes until both sides are crispy and the internal temperature reaches at least 145°F.
Remove and place on a wire rack over a baking sheet to drain excess oil. Repeat with remaining pieces.
Let rest for 2–3 minutes before serving. Serve with lemon wedges if desired.

name: Candy Cane Cheesecake
description: A festive peppermint cheesecake with an Oreo crust, white chocolate filling, and crushed candy canes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F and lightly grease a 9-inch springform pan. Wrap the outside of the pan with foil and place it on a baking sheet to catch any leaks.
Place the Oreo cookies (with filling removed) in a food processor and pulse until fine crumbs form. Transfer to a bowl, add melted butter, and mix until evenly coated.
Press the mixture firmly into the bottom of the springform pan to create an even crust. Use the bottom of a cup to pack it down tightly.
In a microwave-safe bowl, melt the white chocolate in 20–30 second intervals, stirring between each until smooth. Let it cool slightly so it doesn’t scramble the eggs later.
In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth using a hand mixer or stand mixer. Add sugar and mix until fully combined and creamy.
Mix in vanilla extract, peppermint extract, and sour cream until smooth.
Add the eggs and mix on low speed just until combined. Do not overmix to avoid cracks in the cheesecake.
Stir in the melted white chocolate, mini chocolate chips, and crushed candy canes until evenly distributed.
Pour the batter into the prepared crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
Bake on the center rack for 45 minutes, or until the edges are set but the center still has a slight jiggle. The internal temperature should be about 150–155°F.
Turn off the oven, crack the door open slightly, and let the cheesecake cool inside for 1 hour to prevent cracking.
Remove from the oven and refrigerate for at least 6 hours (preferably overnight) before slicing.
Run a knife around the edge before removing the springform ring. Slice and serve chilled.

name: Creamy Macaroni and Cheese
description: A rich and creamy Southern-style mac and cheese made with eggs, cheese, and a slow cooker method.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the elbow macaroni in a 2–3 quart pot for about 7 minutes until just tender (al dente). Drain well and set aside.
In a medium saucepan over medium heat, add butter and cheddar cheese. Stir continuously until the cheese is fully melted and smooth.
Transfer the melted cheese mixture into a slow cooker (4–6 quarts). Add sour cream, cheddar cheese soup, salt, milk, dry mustard, and black pepper. Stir until fully combined.
Add the beaten eggs and mix quickly so they incorporate evenly without scrambling.
Add the cooked macaroni and stir thoroughly so all the noodles are coated in the sauce.
Cover and cook on LOW for 3 hours, stirring about once every hour to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.
Once thick and creamy, turn off the heat and let sit for 5–10 minutes before serving.

name: Roasted Alla Norma Rigatoni
description: A classic Sicilian-style pasta with roasted eggplant, tomatoes, and ricotta.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F and use a large roasting pan or baking sheet so the vegetables have space to roast evenly.
In a bowl, mix the tomato paste, red wine vinegar, and tomatoes until evenly coated. Spread this mixture on one side of the roasting pan.
Place the eggplant on the other side of the pan. Drizzle everything with olive oil, then sprinkle chilli flakes and oregano evenly over the vegetables. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Roast for about 30 minutes, or until the eggplant is soft and the tomatoes have collapsed and released their juices.
While the vegetables are roasting, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the rigatoni and cook according to package directions until al dente (tender but still slightly firm).
Before draining, reserve about 1/3 cup of the pasta cooking water. Drain the pasta.

Remove the roasted vegetables from the oven. Add the cooked pasta directly to the pan and pour in the reserved pasta water. Toss everything together gently until well combined and coated in the sauce.
Transfer to serving bowls and top with crumbled ricotta and fresh basil leaves. Serve immediately.

name: Pan Seared Cod in White Wine Tomato Basil Sauce
description: A quick and flavorful cod dish topped with a fresh tomato basil sauce and white wine.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat. Add red pepper flakes and garlic and cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly until fragrant but not browned.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for 8–12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they soften and begin to blister but still hold their shape.

Pour in the white wine and bring to a gentle simmer. Add basil, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, sugar, and black pepper. Stir and cook for about 2 minutes, then transfer the sauce to a bowl and set aside.
Heat the remaining 2 tbsp olive oil in the same skillet over medium heat. Pat the cod dry with paper towels and season both sides with salt and black pepper.

Place the cod in the skillet and cook for about 3–4 minutes on the first side until golden brown. Carefully flip and cook another 3–4 minutes until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork or reaches 145°F internally.
Pour the prepared tomato basil sauce over the cod and cook for 1 minute to warm everything through.
Remove from heat and serve immediately.

name: Easy Pad Thai
description: A quick and flavorful noodle dish with shrimp, eggs, and a sweet-savory sauce.
instructions: Place the rice noodles in a large bowl and cover with hot water. Let them soak for about 5–7 minutes until softened but still slightly firm, then drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together fish sauce, brown sugar, rice vinegar, and red pepper flakes until the sugar dissolves. Set the sauce aside.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp oil. Once hot, add the shrimp and cook for 2–3 minutes per side until pink and fully cooked. Remove and set aside.
Add another 2 tbsp oil to the same pan, then add garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push everything to one side of the pan and crack the eggs into the empty space. Scramble them for 1–2 minutes until just set, then mix with the garlic.
Add the drained noodles and pour the sauce over them. Toss continuously using tongs or a spatula for 2–3 minutes so the noodles absorb the sauce and don’t stick.
Return the shrimp to the pan and add bean sprouts and green onions. Toss everything together and cook for another 1–2 minutes until heated through.
Remove from heat and sprinkle with crushed peanuts. Serve immediately with lime wedges on the side.

name: Roasted Spiced Chicken and Apples
description: A whole roasted chicken seasoned with warm spices and cooked with apples and herbs.
instructions: Place the chicken breast-side up in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Season the inside and outside evenly with salt and baharat spice. Refrigerate uncovered for at least 1 hour or up to overnight to help the skin crisp.
Preheat oven to 450°F and position a rack in the center.
Stuff the cavity of the chicken with the onion, about half of the thyme, and half of the rosemary.
Arrange the apples around the chicken in the pan. Sprinkle them with sugar and a pinch of salt, then drizzle olive oil over everything.
Place the pan in the oven and roast for about 1 hour, or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 160°F and the juices run clear.
Remove the pan from the oven and let the chicken rest for 10 minutes before carving so the juices redistribute.
Discard the herb sprigs, then add a splash of apple cider vinegar to the apples and gently toss.
Carve the chicken and serve with the roasted apples on the side.

name: Authentic Mexican Rice
description: A classic Mexican side dish made with toasted rice, tomato, and simple seasonings.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine mesh strainer under cold water until the water runs clear. This removes excess starch and helps the rice cook fluffy instead of sticky.

Heat a medium saucepan (3–4 quarts) over medium-high heat and add the oil. Once the oil is hot, add the rice and cook for about 8–10 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice turns lightly golden brown.
Add garlic, onion, and tomato sauce to the pan. Stir well so the rice is evenly coated.
Add tomato bouillon, salt, carrot, peas, and water. Stir to combine and make sure everything is evenly distributed.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat. Once boiling, reduce heat to low, cover with a lid, and let it simmer for about 20 minutes or until all the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pot from heat and let the rice rest, covered, for 5 minutes. This helps it finish steaming and improves texture.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork before serving. Do not stir aggressively or it can become mushy.

name: Lemon Butter Scallops
description: A quick and elegant dish with perfectly seared scallops in a simple garlic lemon butter sauce.
instructions: Pat the scallops dry with paper towels to remove as much moisture as possible. Remove the small side muscle if attached, as it can be tough when cooked.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet (preferably cast iron) over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp butter. Once melted and slightly bubbling, place the scallops in a single layer without overcrowding.
Season the scallops with salt and black pepper. Cook for 2–3 minutes without moving them until a golden crust forms on the bottom.
Flip the scallops and cook for another 2–3 minutes until the centers are opaque and slightly firm. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add 2 tbsp butter to the same skillet. Once melted, add garlic and cook for about 1 minute, stirring frequently until fragrant but not browned.
Add lemon juice and stir, letting it simmer for about 1 minute to combine the flavors.
Return the scallops to the pan and spoon the sauce over them. Cook for about 1 minute to heat through.
Remove from heat and sprinkle with parsley before serving. Serve immediately.

name: Lemon Poppy Seed Muffins
description: Bright, bakery-style muffins with fresh lemon flavor and a light, tender texture.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners or lightly grease each cup so the muffins don’t stick.
In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, poppy seeds, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir until evenly mixed and no clumps remain.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, melted butter, lemon zest, and lemon juice until smooth and fully combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently stir until just combined. Do not overmix—some small lumps are fine and will keep the muffins tender.
Divide the batter evenly into the muffin cups, filling each about 3/4 full so they have room to rise.
Place the muffin tin on the center rack of the oven and bake for 20–25 minutes, or until the tops are lightly golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let the muffins cool in the pan for about 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly.
In a small bowl, whisk together confectioners sugar and lemon juice until smooth to make the glaze.
Drizzle the glaze over the warm muffins and let it set for a few minutes before serving.

name: Lemon Chicken and Vegetable Soup
description: A nourishing soup with tender chicken, vegetables, and a bright lemon finish with a creamy texture from egg yolks.
instructions: Melt the butter in a large pot or Dutch oven (5–6 quarts) over low heat. Add the carrots, celery, onion, zucchini, and garlic. Stir, cover with a lid, and let cook for about 30 minutes so the vegetables soften and release flavor without browning.

Add the chicken breasts, bone broth, salt, and black pepper. Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a boil, then reduce to medium-low and simmer for about 8 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked (internal temperature 165°F).
Remove the chicken and place it on a cutting board. Cut into bite-sized pieces or shred with two forks, then return it to the pot.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and lemon juice until smooth. Slowly add about 1/2 cup of the hot broth into the mixture while whisking constantly to prevent curdling.
Slowly pour the tempered egg mixture back into the soup while stirring constantly. Keep the heat low and do not let the soup boil to maintain a smooth, creamy texture.
Stir in the parsley and let the soup sit for a few minutes before serving so the flavors come together. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.

name: Lemon Chicken Orzo Soup
description: A bright and comforting soup with tender chicken, vegetables, orzo pasta, and fresh lemon.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven (5–6 quarts) over medium heat. Add carrots, celery, onion, basil, salt, and pepper. Cook for about 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and slightly browned.
Add garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant but not burned.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir until it is fully absorbed with no visible dry spots.
Pour in the chicken broth and stir well to combine. Bring the soup to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Carefully add the chicken breasts to the pot. Reduce heat to medium-low and let simmer for 10–15 minutes until the chicken reaches 165°F internally and is fully cooked.
Remove the chicken and place it on a cutting board. Let it rest for 5 minutes, then shred or dice into bite-sized pieces.
Bring the soup back to a simmer and add the orzo. Cook for about 7–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the orzo is just tender but not mushy.
Return the chicken to the pot and stir in the spinach, lemon juice, and dill. Cook for another 3–5 minutes until the spinach is wilted and everything is heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Let sit for a few minutes before serving.

name: Lemon Chicken
description: A crispy parmesan-crusted chicken served with a rich garlic lemon butter sauce.
instructions: Slice the chicken breasts in half lengthwise to create thinner cutlets, then pound them to about 1/2 inch thick so they cook evenly.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, minced garlic, Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper until combined.
In a separate bowl, mix parmesan cheese and flour until evenly combined.
Dip each piece of chicken into the egg mixture, then coat thoroughly in the parmesan mixture, pressing lightly so it sticks.

Heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat and add olive oil. Once hot, add the chicken and cook for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet over medium heat, melt butter and add garlic. Cook for about 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.
Add lemon juice, chicken broth, and black pepper. Stir and let simmer for about 2 minutes until slightly thickened.
Return the chicken to the skillet and spoon the sauce over the top. Cook for 1–2 minutes to heat through.
Remove from heat and garnish with parsley and lemon slices if desired. Let rest for 2–3 minutes before serving.

name: Italian Wedding Soup
description: A comforting soup with mini meatballs, vegetables, pasta, and spinach in a rich broth.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with foil. Place a wire rack on top if available to help the meatballs cook evenly.

In a large bowl, whisk together the egg, chives, sage, and garlic until combined. Add ground beef, sausage, Parmesan, bread crumbs, and salt, then gently mix with your hands until just combined. Do not overmix or the meatballs will be dense.
Roll the mixture into small meatballs about 1 inch in size and place them on the prepared rack or baking sheet. Bake for 15–18 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through, then set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven (5–6 quarts) over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery and cook for about 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add chicken broth, beef broth, water, wine, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Stir and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add the pasta and cook uncovered for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is tender but not mushy.
Reduce heat to low and add the cooked meatballs and spinach. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until the spinach is wilted and the meatballs are heated through.
Remove the bay leaf, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed. Let sit for a few minutes before serving.

name: Homemade Taco Seasoning
description: A simple spice blend that adds smoky, savory flavor to tacos and more.
instructions: Add all ingredients to a small mixing bowl.
Stir thoroughly for about 30–60 seconds until all spices are evenly combined and there are no clumps.
Transfer the seasoning to an airtight container or spice jar for storage.
To use, heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and cook 1 lb ground meat for about 5 minutes until browned. Drain excess fat.
Add 2–3 tbsp of the seasoning to the cooked meat and stir well to coat evenly.
If you want a saucier mixture, add 1/3 cup water and cook for another 3–5 minutes until thickened.

name: Hibachi Chicken
description: A quick and flavorful Japanese steakhouse-style chicken cooked in a hot skillet with a simple savory sauce.
instructions: Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces (about 1-inch chunks) so they cook quickly and evenly. Pat dry with paper towels to help them brown better.
Heat a large 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, add the chicken in a single layer.
Cook the chicken for 4–5 minutes without stirring too much so it develops a golden brown crust, then flip and cook another 3–4 minutes until mostly cooked through.
Add butter, garlic, and ginger to the pan. Stir and cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant but not burned.
Add soy sauce, lemon juice, and sesame oil. Stir everything together and cook for another 2–3 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked and coated in the sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Remove from heat and let sit for 1–2 minutes before serving.

name: Grilled Moroccan Chicken
description: A flavorful grilled chicken marinated in warm Moroccan spices.
instructions: Place the chicken breasts between two pieces of plastic wrap or parchment paper and pound them to about 1/2 inch thickness using a meat mallet or rolling pin so they cook evenly.
In a large bowl or zip-top bag, combine olive oil, salt, sugar, garlic, paprika, cumin, coriander, ginger, turmeric, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper. Mix well until fully combined.
Add the chicken to the marinade and turn or massage it until all pieces are evenly coated. Cover or seal and refrigerate for at least 5 hours or overnight for best flavor.
Preheat a grill to high heat (about 450–500°F). Clean and lightly oil the grates to prevent sticking.
Place the chicken on the grill and cook for 2–3 minutes per side with the lid closed, until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Remove from the grill and let rest for 3–5 minutes before serving so the juices settle.

name: Greek Shrimp Saganaki
description: A classic Greek dish of shrimp baked in a rich tomato sauce with feta cheese and herbs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F and place a rack in the center of the oven. Use a large oven-safe skillet (10–12 inch) for best results.
Heat olive oil in the skillet over medium-low heat. Add the shallots and garlic and cook for 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned.
Add the diced tomatoes with their juices, salt, black pepper, cumin, red pepper flakes, and honey. Stir well and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the sauce simmer uncovered for 15–20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Remove the skillet from heat and arrange the shrimp in a single layer over the sauce.
Sprinkle the feta cheese evenly over the shrimp, then sprinkle the dried oregano on top.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink, opaque, and fully cooked through.
Switch the oven to broil and cook for 1–2 minutes until the feta is lightly golden on top. Watch closely so it doesn’t burn.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with fresh mint before serving. Let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving.

name: French Fry Seasoning
description: A simple homemade spice blend that adds bold flavor to fries and other dishes.
instructions: Add all ingredients to a small mixing bowl.
Stir thoroughly for about 30–60 seconds until all spices are evenly combined and there are no clumps.
Transfer the seasoning to an airtight container or spice jar for storage.
To use, sprinkle over hot fries immediately after cooking so the seasoning sticks better.

name: Beef and Broccoli
description: A simple takeout-style beef and broccoli with a savory homemade sauce.

ingredients.name: carrots
ingredients.prepNote: halved and cut into long slices

instructions: Slice the steak into very thin strips (about 1/4 inch thick) against the grain so it stays tender when cooked.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, chili garlic sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, sherry, water, and cornstarch until smooth and fully combined.

Heat a 10–12 inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add about 1 tbsp oil. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, add the steak in a single layer and cook for 2–3 minutes per side until browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add a little more oil if needed, then add onion, carrots, and garlic. Cook for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened and fragrant.
Add the broccoli and cook for another 3–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until bright green and slightly tender but still crisp.
Pour the sauce into the pan and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Cook for 2–3 minutes until the sauce begins to thicken.
Return the steak to the pan and stir everything together. Cook for another 2–3 minutes until the beef is fully cooked and the sauce coats everything evenly.
Remove from heat and let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving so the sauce thickens slightly more. Serve hot over rice if desired.

name: Chimichurri Sauce
description: A bright, fresh herb sauce perfect for grilled meats with garlic, vinegar, and olive oil.
instructions: Remove any thick stems from the parsley and basil, then rinse the herbs in a colander and shake off excess water.
Finely chop the parsley, basil, garlic, and onion using a sharp knife or food processor. Do not over-blend—the mixture should be finely chopped, not a paste.
Place all chopped ingredients into a medium mixing bowl. Add olive oil, balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, paprika, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Stir everything together thoroughly until well combined. The mixture should look loose and oily with evenly distributed herbs.
Let the sauce sit at room temperature for at least 10–15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to blend together.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed by adding more salt, vinegar, or oil depending on preference.
Serve immediately over grilled meat, chicken, or vegetables, or store in the refrigerator and bring back to room temperature before using.

name: Mongolian Beef
description: A quick takeout-style beef dish with a sweet and savory garlic soy sauce.
instructions: Slice the flank steak into very thin pieces about 1/4 inch thick, cutting against the grain so the meat stays tender. Place the slices in a bowl and toss with cornstarch until evenly coated, then shake off any excess.
Heat a 10-inch skillet over medium-low heat and add 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once warm, add the ginger and garlic and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.

Add soy sauce, water, and brown sugar to the skillet. Stir well and bring to a gentle boil over medium heat. Let it cook for 3–5 minutes until the sauce slightly thickens, then remove from heat and set aside.

Heat a separate 10–12 inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the remaining oil. Once the oil is hot (shimmering but not smoking), add the beef in small batches so the pan is not overcrowded.
Cook each batch for about 2 minutes, flipping once, until lightly browned but not fully cooked through. Remove and repeat with remaining beef.
Return all the cooked beef to the pan and pour the prepared sauce over it. Cook over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring, until the sauce becomes thick and coats the beef evenly.
Remove from heat and stir in the sliced green onions. Serve immediately while hot.

name: Doctored Cake Mix Cake
description: A simple upgraded cake mix recipe that turns a boxed mix into a richer, more moist cake.

ingredients.name: instant espresso granules, dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease two 9-inch round cake pans or a 9x13 inch baking dish.
In a large mixing bowl, add the cake mix, pudding mix, sour cream, vegetable oil, eggs, vanilla extract, and espresso mixture.
Use a hand mixer or stand mixer on medium speed for about 2 minutes until the batter is smooth and fully combined.
Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pans and spread it out with a spatula so the tops are level.
Place the pans in the center rack of the oven and bake for 25–30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let the cakes cool in the pans for about 10–15 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before frosting or serving.

name: Mushroom Spinach Crustless Quiche
description: A simple, protein-packed quiche with sautéed vegetables and cheese—great for breakfast or meal prep.

ingredients.name: olive oil, plus more for greasing pan

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and lightly grease a 9-inch pie dish with olive oil. Place the dish on a baking sheet to catch spills.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and milk until fully combined and slightly frothy, about 30–45 seconds.

Heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Once the oil is shimmering, add the mushrooms, onion, and garlic. Cook for 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms release their moisture and the onions become soft and translucent.
Add thyme, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, then drizzle in the balsamic vinegar and stir. Cook for another 1–2 minutes.
Add the spinach and cook for about 2–3 minutes, stirring until fully wilted and reduced in size. Remove from heat.
Spread the cooked vegetable mixture evenly into the prepared pie dish. Sprinkle the cubed cheese over the top.
Pour the egg mixture evenly over everything, filling the dish close to the top.
Carefully place the baking sheet in the oven and bake for 35 minutes, or until the center is set and no longer jiggles when gently shaken.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before slicing so it holds its shape better.

name: Slow Cooker Korean Beef
description: Tender, shredded beef cooked low and slow in a sweet, savory, and slightly spicy Korean-inspired sauce with garlic, ginger, and gochujang. (Prep time not clearly stated)

ingredients.name: Beef Chuck Roast
ingredients.prepNote: Fat trimmed and cut into 1.5" pieces

instructions: In a 6-quart slow cooker, add the ginger, garlic, beef broth, brown sugar, soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and cornstarch. Whisk everything together directly in the slow cooker until smooth and fully combined.
Cut the beef chuck roast into large chunks (about 2–3 inches each) and place them into the slow cooker. Turn the pieces using tongs so they are fully coated in the sauce.
Cover with the lid and cook on HIGH for 5–6 hours or until the beef is very tender and easily pulls apart with a fork.
Once cooked, turn off the slow cooker and let it sit uncovered for about 10–15 minutes so the sauce can settle and cool slightly.
Use a spoon to skim off excess fat from the surface of the sauce, leaving a small amount for flavor.
Remove the beef and place it on a cutting board or large bowl. Use two forks to shred the meat into bite-sized pieces.
Return the shredded beef to the slow cooker and stir it into the sauce until evenly coated.
Let it sit in the warm sauce for 5–10 minutes so it absorbs more flavor.
Serve warm over rice or with vegetables, spooning extra sauce over the top.

name: Slow Cooker Flank Steak
description: A tender, flavorful flank steak slow-cooked in a savory-sweet marinade—perfect for an easy, hands-off dinner.
instructions: In a small mixing bowl, combine the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, honey, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Whisk for about 30–45 seconds until fully combined.
Place the flank steak into a 6-quart slow cooker (or similar size). Pour the marinade over the steak. Use tongs or a spoon to flip the steak 1–2 times so it is fully coated in the liquid.

Cover the slow cooker with the lid. Cook on LOW for 6 hours (recommended for most tender results) or HIGH for 3–4 hours. The steak is done when it is very tender and can be easily pulled apart with a fork.
Carefully remove the steak from the slow cooker and place it on a cutting board. Let it rest for 5–10 minutes so the juices redistribute and the meat stays moist.
Using a sharp knife, slice the steak against the grain into thin strips (this makes it much more tender and easier to chew).
Serve warm with the juices from the slow cooker spooned over the top for extra flavor.

name: Cowboy Caviar
description: A fresh, colorful bean and vegetable dip tossed in a zesty homemade dressing—great as a dip with chips or as a side salad.
instructions: Rinse and drain the black beans and black eyed peas thoroughly using a colander. Shake off excess water so the dip doesn’t become watery.
Dice the tomatoes, jalapeño, bell pepper, and red onion into small, even pieces (about ¼-inch size). Chop the cilantro and avocado last so it stays fresh.
In a large mixing bowl (at least 3–4 quarts), combine the beans, black eyed peas, tomatoes, jalapeño, corn, bell pepper, red onion, and cilantro.
In a small bowl, add the olive oil, lime juice, honey, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Whisk for about 30 seconds until fully combined and slightly thickened.
Pour the dressing over the bean mixture. Use a large spoon to gently toss everything together until evenly coated.
Add the chopped avocado and gently fold it in last to avoid smashing it.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed (add more salt, lime juice, or spice depending on preference).
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to let the flavors blend together (optional but recommended).
Serve chilled or at room temperature in a large bowl with tortilla chips, or use as a topping for grilled meats.

name: Fried Cornmeal Mush
description: A simple, old-fashioned dish made from cornmeal that’s chilled, sliced, and fried until crispy on the outside and soft inside—often served with butter or syrup. (Prep time not clearly stated) (Cook time not clearly stated — includes chilling time)
instructions: Lightly grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan (or similar small baking dish) and set aside.
In a medium saucepan (about 2–3 quarts), bring 2¾ cups of water to a boil over medium-high heat. You should see steady bubbling, not just light steam.
In a bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, 1 cup cold water, salt, and sugar until completely smooth with no lumps.

Once the water is boiling, slowly pour in the cornmeal mixture while stirring constantly with a spoon or whisk. Keep the heat at medium and stir continuously to prevent lumps. The mixture will begin to thicken within 2–3 minutes.
When it reaches a thick, smooth, porridge-like consistency, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring every 1–2 minutes to prevent sticking or burning on the bottom.

Pour the thickened mixture into the prepared loaf pan and spread it evenly using a spatula. Let it cool at room temperature for about 15–20 minutes, then transfer to the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or until completely firm. It should feel solid and hold its shape when touched.
Remove from the pan and slice into ½ to 1-inch thick pieces using a sharp knife. Try to keep slices even so they cook evenly.
Heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat and add the butter. Once the butter is melted and lightly bubbling (but not browning), add the slices in a single layer.
Cook each side for 4–5 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy. Flip carefully using a spatula. The outside should be crisp while the inside stays soft.
Serve warm with maple syrup, butter, or bacon bits if desired.

name: Corn Chowder
description: A creamy, hearty corn chowder loaded with sweet corn, tender potatoes, and bacon in a rich, flavorful broth.
instructions:
Prep the corn: 
Cut the kernels off the corn cobs using a knife. Set the kernels aside and keep the empty cobs—you’ll use them for extra flavor.

Make the base broth:
In a large pot, add the chicken broth, milk, and heavy cream. Place the corn cobs into the liquid and bring it to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Let it cook for about 15–20 minutes to infuse flavor, then remove and discard the cobs.

Cook the bacon:
In a separate large pot or Dutch oven, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy (about 5–7 minutes). Remove the bacon and place it on a paper towel to drain, leaving the fat in the pot.

Sauté the vegetables:
Dice the onion, carrot, and celery into small pieces. Add them to the pot with the bacon fat and cook over medium heat for about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and slightly translucent.

Add potatoes and corn:
Peel and dice the potatoes into bite-sized chunks. Add them to the pot along with the corn kernels. Stir everything together.

Season and combine:
Add salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Pour in the prepared broth mixture. Stir well to combine.

Simmer until tender:
Bring the soup to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and let it simmer uncovered for about 15–20 minutes, or until the potatoes are fork-tender (you should be able to easily pierce them).

Finish and serve:
Chop the cooked bacon into small pieces. Taste the soup and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot, topped with bacon and chopped chives.

name: Beef Stew
description: This classic beef stew recipe is hearty, versatile, and freezer-friendly—a cozy one-pot meal that warms you from the inside out.

ingredients.name: boneless beef chuck, (well-marbled, cut into 1½-inch pieces)
large carrots, (peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks on a diagonal)
small white boiling potatoes ((baby yukons), cut in half)

instructions: Preheat oven (325) and season beef with salt and pepper.
Brown beef in batches in oil; remove and set aside.
Cook onions, garlic, and balsamic vinegar; stir in tomato paste.
Return beef, add flour, and stir.
Add wine, broth, water, and seasonings; bring to a boil.
Cover and cook in oven for about 2 hours.
Add carrots and potatoes.
Cook 1 more hour until tender.

name: Chicken Tetrazzini
description: My Chicken Tetrazzini recipe will quickly become one of your favorite casseroles! It's creamy and cheesy, and so easy to make. I made it even easier by using rotisserie chicken, but you'll find directions on how to swap baked and chopped chicken in the notes below. This recipe is freezer-friendly and can be made ahead by storing it in the fridge before baking.

ingredients.name: rotisserie chicken (shredded or diced, or cook 1.5 lbs of chicken breast*)
half & half (or sub with equal parts milk; heavy cream)

instructions: Preheat Oven to 350˚F and butter a 9x13 Casserole dish.

Cook pasta in a large pot of salted boiling water (it should taste like ocean water) and cook until barely al dente according to package instructions - do not overcook. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta, partially cover, and set aside.

Saute the Veggies: Place a large skillet over medium/high heat. Add oil then add sliced mushrooms and sauté 3 min or until softened. Add diced onions and cook until onions are soft and golden, 5-7 min. Add minced garlic and sauté another 1 min until fragrant. Transfer to the cutting board with chicken.

Make the Sauce: In the same pot, melt butter and whisk in flour until lightly golden (1 1/2 min). Slowly whisk in chicken broth, lemon juice, salt, and pepper and whisk until smooth. Add half and half and bring to a simmer. Season the sauce with more salt and pepper to taste.

Combine the Casserole: Into the pasta pot, add the chicken, mushrooms mixture, parsley, and all of your sauce. Stir to combine. You can add 1/2 cup of reserved pasta water to make it saucier then spread it evenly into your prepared casserole dish.  Sprinkle the top generously with mozzarella.
To Bake: Cover with foil and bake at 350˚F for 20 min, then remove the lid and continue baking for 15 min. You can broil for the last 1-2 minutes to get some golden spots on top.

name: Chicken Salad
description: Craving that Chicken Salad Chick Classic Carol Chicken Salad? You’re in luck! No need for fancy ingredients—just tender white meat chicken, celery, mayo, and a hint of my secret seasoning blend. Make your own Chicken Salad Chick recipe right at home!

ingredients.name: shredded chicken (all-white meat or rotisserie chicken. Small shreds work best. See notes.)

instructions: Add the chicken, celery, mayonnaise, vinegar, and seasonings in a large mixing bowl. Stir until well combined.
Taste and season with additional salt and pepper if desired.
Allow the chicken salad to chill in the fridge for at least 30 minutes for optimal flavor. May be served immediately of course!

name: Chicken Parmesan
description: Classic crispy chicken breasts topped with marinara sauce and melted cheese—an Italian-American favorite. Rao's Sauce suggested with angel hair pasta.

ingredients.name: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
Place chicken breasts between plastic wrap and pound to even thickness. Season with salt and pepper.
Prepare three bowls: flour, beaten eggs, and a mixture of bread crumbs + Parmesan.
Dredge chicken in flour, dip in eggs, then coat with breadcrumb mixture.
Heat olive oil in a pan and cook chicken until golden on both sides.
Transfer to a baking dish. Spoon tomato sauce over each piece and top with mozzarella, provolone, and basil.
Drizzle with olive oil.
Bake until cheese is melted and bubbly and chicken is cooked through (about 15–20 minutes).

name: Lady & Sons Beef Vegetable Soup
description: Favorite Beef veggie soup

ingredients.name: Beef bone
ingredients.prepNote: if don't have beef bone, get a couple of beef short ribs to add flavor

instructions: If using chuck roast, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Place the roast in the skillet and cook until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the roast from the skillet and cut it into 1 1/2 to 2-inch cubes; discard the fat. Place the beef cubes in a large stockpot. (If using short ribs, you can put them right in the pot with no preparation).

Add the water, seasonings, onion and tomatoes and bring to a boil over high heat. Cover the pot; reduce the heat so that the liquid simmers, and cook for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until the meat is very tender. If using short ribs, remove them from the pot and cut the meat from the bones, discard the bones and fat, and return the meat to the pot. Add the remaining vegetables and the macaroni and return the soup to a boil, stirring to distribute the ingredients. Reduce the heat and simmer for 45 minutes. Just before serving, add fresh parsley to pot. To remove excess fat from the surface of the soup, swirl a lettuce leaf around the surface-it will pick up a lot of the fat.
The chuck roast will yield more meat, but the bones from the short ribs give the soup an incredible flavor.

name: Beef tips with Stew Meat
description: tender and easy beef tips

ingredients.name: (.75 (.75 ounce) packet dry brown gravy mix

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add onion and saute until almost translucent, about 3 minutes. Add stew meat and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides; about 3 to 5 minutes.

Pour 2 cups water, soy sauce, and Worcestershire sauce into the skillet. Stir in garlic powder, salt, and pepper; bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer until meat is tender, 1 1/2 to 2 hours.

When the meat is almost finished, mix remaining 1 cup water and gravy mix until thoroughly combined; stir into the meat mixture and bring to a boil. Cook, stirring frequently, until slightly thickened.

name: Chicken Fricassee with Apples
description: With its blend of sweet apples and savory chicken, this fricassee is just what you need for a comforting fall dinner.

ingredients.name: Â½ pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts (2 large), or chicken tenderloins (see note)
Honeycrisp, Fuji, or Gala apples, cored and cut into Â¼-inch (6-mm) thick slices (do not peel)
minced fresh thyme (or Â¾ teaspoon dried)

instructions: Slice the chicken breasts in half horizontally to form four flat fillets. (Skip this step if using chicken tenderloins; instead, using the palm of your hand, gently flatten the tenderloins so that they are an even Â½-inch/1-cm thickness.)

Pat the chicken dry with paper towels and season all over with 1 teaspoon salt and Â½ teaspoon pepper. Spread the flour in a shallow dish. Dredge the chicken in the flour to coat, shaking to remove excess; transfer to a plate and set aside.

Melt 1 tablespoon of the butter with 1 tablespoon of the oil in a 12-inch (30-cm) nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the apples to the pan and season with Â¼ teaspoon salt and Â¼ teaspoon pepper. Cook the apples, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned and softened, 6 to 8 minutes. Transfer to a second plate; set aside.

Melt the remaining 1 tablespoon butter with the remaining tablespoon oil in the now-empty skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until lightly browned but not quite cooked through, about 2 minutes per side. Set the partially cooked chicken on another plate and set aside.
Add the onion, thyme, a heaping Â¼ teaspoon salt, and Â¼ teaspoon pepper to now-empty skillet and cook over medium heat until the onion is softened and browned, 4 to 5 minutes.
Add the cider and broth to the skillet, increase the heat to high, and bring the mixture to a boil. Cook, uncovered, until the liquid is reduced by approximately half, about 5 minutes.

Add the cream and bring to a boil. Cook until sauce has thickened slightly, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the chicken and any accumulated juices back to the skillet, reduce the heat to low, and simmer, uncovered, until the chicken is cooked through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in the vinegar and apples. Taste the sauce and adjust the seasoning, if necessary. Spoon the sauce and apples over the chicken, sprinkle with the chives, and serve.

I buy two large chicken breasts and slice them in half horizontally, which makes four nice portions that cook quickly in one batch. I don't recommend using the pre-sliced chicken breasts sold at the supermarket â they are typically very thin, which means you'd need more of them to get 1Â½ pounds (680 g), and you'd have to cook the chicken in two batches. If you don't want to bother with slicing the chicken breasts yourself, chicken tenderloins are a great alternative.

name: The Best Chocolate Cake Recipe {Ever}
description: The Best Chocolate Cake Recipe - A one bowl chocolate cake recipe that is quick, easy, and delicious! Updated with gluten-free, dairy-free, and egg-free options!

ingredients.name: milk (or buttermilk, almond, or coconut milk)
vegetable oil (or canola oil, or melted coconut oil)

instructions: Preheat oven to 350Âº F. Prepare two 9-inch cake pans by spraying with baking spray, buttering and lightly flouring, or brushing them with homemade chocolate pan release.

Add flour, sugar, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, salt and espresso powder to a large bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer. Whisk through to combine or, using your paddle attachment, stir through flour mixture until combined well.
Add milk, vegetable oil, eggs, and vanilla to flour mixture and mix together on medium speed until well combined. Reduce speed and carefully add boiling water to the cake batter until well combined.
Distribute cake batter evenly between the two prepared cake pans. Bake for 30-35 minutes, until a toothpick or cake tester inserted in the center of the chocolate cake comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and allow to cool for about 10 minutes, remove from the pan and cool completely.
Frost the cake with Chocolate Buttercream Frosting.

name: Chicken and Broccoli (Chinese Takeout Style)
description: An easy chicken and broccoli stir fry recipe that yields juicy chicken and crisp broccoli in a rich brown sauce, just like the one at a Chinese restaurant.
instructions: Slice the chicken against the grain into thin bite-size pieces, no thicker than 1/4â (1/2 cm), transfer into a medium-size bowl. Add the marinade ingredients. Stir to mix well. Let sit for 10 minutes while preparing other ingredients.
Combine all the sauce ingredients in a bowl. Stir to mix well.

Bring 1/3 cup of water to a boil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the broccoli and cover. Steam until the broccoli just turns tender and the water evaporates, about 40 to 50 seconds. Transfer the broccoli to a plate. Wipe the pan with a paper towel held in a pair of tongs if thereâs any water left.

Add the oil and swirl to coat the bottom. Spread the chicken in the skillet in a single layer. Allow to cook without touching for 30 seconds, or until the bottom side is browned. Flip to cook the other side for a few seconds. Stir and cook until the surface is lightly charred and the inside is still pink.
Add the garlic and ginger. Stir a few times to release the flavor and fragrance.

Return the broccoli to the pan. Stir the sauce again to dissolve the cornstarch completely and pour it into the skillet. Cook and stir until the sauce thickens, about 1 minute. Transfer everything to a plate immediately.
Serve hot with steamed rice or boiled noodles as a main dish.

name: Maple Chicken Breakfast Sausage â Low Carb, Paleo, Gluten Free
description: Good breakfast sausage

ingredients.name: Tbs. Maple Syrup - (Low Carb Sugar-Free Option - Here)

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground chicken, maple syrup, salt, rubbed sage, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, thyme, red pepper flakes, nutmeg, and cloves.
Mix until all ingredients are well-incorporated
Form into 2 oz. patties. I like to roll them into balls and then flatten them into patties.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Lightly oil the skillet.
Once the oil is heated, place the sausage patties in the pan. Cook until browned on both sides and cooked all the way through.
Serve and enjoy!!

name: Julia Child Beef Stroganoff Recipe
description: Julia Child Beef Stroganoff is a classic, sophisticated dish featuring seared beef tenderloin strips in a rich sauce of mustard, cream, and mushrooms. It creates a velvety, savory dinner that is surprisingly quick to make but tastes elegant enough for company.

ingredients.name: Tb minced shallots or green onions
Â½ lbs beef tenderloin, trimmed
ingredients.prepNote: Can also use Costco cap-off sirloin

ingredients.name: Â¼ cup Madeira (or dry white vermouth)

instructions: Brown the mushrooms:Â Heat butter and oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and sautÃ© 4-5 minutes until lightly browned.
Add shallots:Â Stir in shallots and cook 1 minute more. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a side dish.
Step 2: Prep and Sear the Beef
Slice the beef:Â Cut tenderloin into pieces about 2 inches across and Â½ inch thick. Dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Sear in batches:Â Heat butter and oil in the same skillet. When the foam subsides, add beef in a single layer. Don't crowd the pan. Sear 2-3 minutes per side. The goal is brown exterior, rosy red interior.
Set aside:Â Transfer beef to a plate. Discard the fat from the skillet.
Deglaze:Â Add Madeira and stock to the skillet. Boil rapidly, scraping up the browned bits, until reduced to about â cup.
Add cream:Â Beat in the whipping cream, then the cornstarch mixture. Simmer for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms:Â Return the mushrooms to the sauce and simmer 1 minute more. The sauce should be lightly thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Return the beef:Â Season the beef lightly with salt and pepper. Add it back to the skillet along with any juices.
Warm gently:Â Cover and heat to just below a simmer for 3-4 minutes. Be very careful not to overdo it or the beef will be well done rather than rare.
Finish with butter:Â Off heat, swirl in the softened butter. Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
notes:
Category: Dinner,Main
Notes: cook meat in batches and don't crowd the pan. 
I like to serve over rice and usually make green beans or asparagus

name: Baked Ziti
description: Yummy pasta dish, great for a crowd

ingredients.name: Sweet Italian Sausage
ingredients.prepNote: brown and drain on paper towels in a bowl

ingredients.name: Pasta noodles
ingredients.prepNote: I use either rigatoni, ziti or penne noodles

ingredients.name: Shredded Parmesan
ingredients.prepNote: I don't measure, just sprinkle on each layer, to taste

ingredients.name: Shredded Mozzarella
ingredients.prepNote: I don't measure, just sprinkle on each layer, to taste

ingredients.name: Fresh Basil
ingredients.prepNote: chopped and sprinkle on each layer

instructions: Brown the ground beef in a large saucepan,  drain and hold to the side.
Brown the Italian sausage, drain on paper towels in a bowl.
Put meat back in pot and then add the 2 jars of Prego. Add sugar and let this simmer for 2 hours or so.
After sauce has cooked for a couple hours or so, boil noodles and chop the basil. Drain the noodles and set aside. Turn oven on to 425

Spoon about a cup of the sauce on the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch (23 x 33-cm) baking dish. Layer 1/3 of the noodles and spread 1c or so of sauce over the noodles. Sprinkle with shredded mozzarella (~1 cup/113 g) and the parmesan, sprinkle some basil. Then repeat this process two more times. Transfer to the oven and bake, uncovered, until the cheese has melted and browned, 15 to 20 minutes. Sprinkle with more basil and serve.

name: Beef Tips and Gravy
description: Learn how to make Beef Tips and Gravy from scratch in one pot on the stove top or in the Slow Cooker! This classic comfort food is great with mashed potatoes, egg noodles, or rice.
instructions: Combine beef seasoning mix and measure out gravy ingredients before beginning.
Cut meat into 1-inch cubes, discard any large pieces of fat.
Combine beef seasoning and sprinkle it over the meat. Toss to coat. Sprinkle flour over the meat and toss again.

Heat 3 Tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the meat in batches, donât overcrowd the pan. Brown on each side for 30-45 seconds. Add a splash of more oil if needed. Transfer to a plate. Repeat with remaining beef. *Don't cook the beef all the way through. You want it to cook in the gravy to add more flavor.

Add ¼ cup wine and use a silicone spatula to âcleanâ (deglaze) the bottom of the pan, removing any brown remnants. The brown specks are called âfondâ and will give the sauce lots of good flavor.
Let the wine gently bubble and reduce by half. Reduce heat to medium and add the butter and the onions. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add all remaining gravy ingredients except the cornstarch mixture. Bring to a boil.
Combine the corn starch and cold water. Whisk into the sauce, a little at a time. Stir continuously. Continue until your desired consistency is obtained.
Reduce heat to medium-low and add the beef along with the juices from the plate. Cook with the lid partially covered for 10-15 minutes, until beef is cooked through. Remove from heat.
Optional: Swirl in 1 Tablespoon of COLD butter at the end, this is called âMonter au Beurreâ and it adds a smooth, velvety finish to sauces and gravies.
Serve with mashed potatoes, egg noodles, or over rice!

name: Beef Tenderloin
description: Anyone can make restaurant-quality Beef Tenderloin Roast from home and I& l walk you through it step-by-step! It;s easy, incredibly tender and full of flavor.

ingredients.name: -5 pound Beef tenderloin* (, trimmed and tied*)

instructions: Trim and Truss Meat (your butcher can do this, and tie it): Remove fat and any silver skin from the meat. Fold the tail (tapered-end) under and use kitchen twine to tie the meat in 1-inch sections. (Depending on your pan size, you may want to cut the beef tenderloin in half, so you have two equal size pieces to fit in a cast-iron skillet, Dutch-oven, roasting pan*, or on a baking sheet).

Air Chill Meat (Optional, but recommended): 10-24 hours before cooking, pat the meat dry with paper towels and season on all sides with kosher salt and black pepper. Place on a wire rack set over a lined baking sheet and allow to chill, uncovered, in the fridge for 10-24 hours. This simple step infuses flavor and will help yield a wonderful crust on the meat. If you donât have time, skip this step.
Make Herb Butter: Combine all ingredients and set aside until ready to use (can be made up to 2 weeks ahead, stored in the fridge).

Prepare Beef Tenderloin: Remove roast from the fridge 1 to 2 hours before cooking, to allow meat to come to room temperature and cook evenly. If you did not air chill the meat, pat the meat with paper towels all over, then season on all sides with salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 425 degrees F.

Sear Meat: Heat oil in a large cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the prepared beef tenderloin, rotating it slowly to sear for a few minutes on each side, to create a golden-brown crust (about 10 minutes).
Brush herb butter all over the meat.
